help.title: Help
help.getHelpWiki: Search for content in the wiki
help.getHelpArchWiki: Search for content in the Arch Wiki
help.aboutBody: Garuda Toolbox is a system maintenance and diagnostics tool for Garuda Linux. It is designed to help users maintain their system and diagnose issues. It is built using Electron and Angular.
help.callExorcistBody: You must be pretty desperate to click this button! I set up some special voodoo for you, if you're still having issues with the app, please report them on the Garuda Linux forum or our issue tracker.

diagnostics.title: Debug info
diagnostics.subtitle: Find the information you need to debug your system
diagnostics.failedCmd: Something bad happened and no diagnostics could be retrieved
diagnostics.uploadSuccess: Successfully uploaded to PrivateBin, URL copied to clipboard

welcome.title: Welcome to
welcome.subtitle: The ultimate Linux distro for gaming and multimedia
welcome.maintenanceSub: Keep your system in a good state
welcome.installSub: Get Garuda Linux on your system
welcome.gitlabSub: Have a look at our source code
welcome.chaoticSub: Take a look at package updates and more
welcome.searxngSub: Search engine that respects your privacy

gaming.title: Gaming
gaming.subtitle: Let's get you started with some gaming apps!
gaming.bluez: Generally required for recognizing PS controllers wireless
gaming.xboxdrv: Xbox controller driver, only use if kernel driver doesn't work!
gaming.wineMeta: Meta package for installing WINE and all required dependencies
gaming.bottles: Easily manage wine and proton prefix
gaming.gamehub: Games manager, downloader, library that supports GOG, Steam and Humble Bundle
gaming.heroic: An Open source Launcher for Epic, Amazon and GOG Games
gaming.itch: Installer for the itch.io desktop app
gaming.minigalaxy: A simple GOG client for Linux
gaming.steamNative: Native replacement for the Steam runtime using system libraries
gaming.gamemode: A daemon/lib combo that allows games to request a set of optimisations be temporarily applied to the host OS
gaming.mangohud: A Vulkan overlay layer for monitoring FPS, temperatures, CPU/GPU load and more
gaming.steamtinkerlaunch: Wrapper script for Steam custom launch options
gaming.vkbasalt: A Vulkan post-processing layer. Some of the effects are CAS, FXAA, SMAA, deband
gaming.dxvk: A Vulkan-based compatibility layer for Direct3D 9/10/11 which allows running 3D applications on Linux using Wine
gaming.gamescopeSession: Gamescope session for compositing window management
gaming.gamescopeSessionSteam: Gamescope session optimized for Steam gaming
gaming.boxtron: Compatibility tool to run DOS games on Steam through native Linux DOSBox
gaming.luxtorpeda: Steam Play compatibility tool to run games using native Linux engines
gaming.protonGeCustom: A fancy custom distribution of Valves Proton with various patches
gaming.protonCachyOS: Distribution of Valves Proton with CachyOS patches and other optimizations
gaming.protontricks: A simple wrapper that does winetricks things for Proton enabled games
gaming.protonupQt: Install and manage Proton-GE and Luxtorpeda for Steam and Wine-GE for Lutris
gaming.winePackage: A compatibility layer for running Windows programs
gaming.wineStaging: A compatibility layer for running Windows programs - Staging branch
gaming.winetricks: Script to install various redistributable runtime libraries in Wine
gaming.antimicrox: Graphical program used to map keyboard buttons & mouse controls to a gamepad
gaming.discord: All-in-one voice and text chat for gamers
gaming.displaycal: Open Source Display Calibration and Characterization powered by Argyll CMS
gaming.droidcam: A tool to turn your phone/tablet into a wireless/usb webcam
gaming.goverlay: A GUI to help manage Vulkan/OpenGL overlays
gaming.gwe: A system utility for controlling NVIDIA GPUs
gaming.gameconqueror: Memory scanner designed to isolate the address of an arbitrary variable in an executing process
gaming.inputRemapper: A tool to change and program the mapping of your input device buttons
gaming.keyboardVisualizer: Turn your OpenRGB-supported RGB setup into a spectacular light show for your music
gaming.mumble: An Open Source, low-latency, high quality voice chat software
gaming.noisetorch: Real-time microphone noise suppression on Linux
gaming.nyrna: Suspend games and applications at any time and resume whenever you wish
gaming.obsStudio: Free, open source software for live streaming and recording
gaming.openrgb: Open source RGB lighting control that doesn't depend on manufacturer software
gaming.oversteer: Graphical application to configure Logitech Wheels
gaming.piper: GTK application to configure gaming mice
gaming.polychromatic: RGB lighting management front-end application for OpenRazer
gaming.scController: User-mode driver, mapper and GTK3 based GUI for Steam Controller, DS4 and similar controllers
gaming.soundwire: Wirelessly transmit any audio from your PC to your Android phone or other PCs
gaming.streamlinkTwitchGui: A multi platform Twitch.tv browser for Streamlink
gaming.vlc: Multi-platform MPEG, VCD/DVD, and DivX player
gaming.xoneDkms: Modern Linux driver for Xbox One and Xbox Series X|S controllers
gaming.xpadneo: Advanced Linux Driver for Xbox One Wireless Gamepad
gaming.dgenSdl: An emulator for Sega Genesis/Mega Drive systems ported to SDL
gaming.dosbox: Emulator with builtin DOS for running DOS Games
gaming.emulationstation: Emulation Station is a flexible emulator front-end supporting keyboardless navigation and custom system themes
gaming.fsUae: An Amiga emulator based on UAE/WinUAE with a focus on emulating games
gaming.hatari: An Atari ST and STE emulator
gaming.kegaFusion: An emulator of classic Sega consoles, including SMS/GG, Genesis/Megadrive and add-ons
gaming.libretroMeta: Meta-package for all supported Libretro emulators
gaming.retroarch: Reference frontend for the libretro API
gaming.mame: Port of the popular Multiple Arcade Machine Emulator using SDL with OpenGL support
gaming.ppsspp: A PSP emulator written in C++
gaming.pegasusFrontend: A cross platform, customizable graphical frontend for launching emulators and managing your game collection
gaming.ryujinx: Experimental Nintendo Switch Emulator written in C#
gaming.sameboy: An accuracy-focused Game Boy/Game Boy Color emulator
gaming.scummvm: A 'virtual machine' for several classic graphical point-and-click adventure games
gaming.snes9x: Portable Super Nintendo Entertainment System (TM) emulator
gaming.waydroid: Python Script to add OpenGapps, Magisk, libhoudini translation library and libndk translation library to waydroid
gaming.mgba: A Nintendo Gameboy Advance Emulator focusing on speed and accuracy

maintenance.title: System
maintenance.subtitle: Keep your system in a good state
maintenance.onlyDirect: This action can not be queued and needs to be run directly.
maintenance.taskRunning: There is already a task running, wait for it to be done first.
maintenance.reinstallPackagesSub: Reinstall all packages on your system
maintenance.btrfsAssistantSub: Manage your BTRFS snapshots and run maintenance tasks
maintenance.updateRemoteFullFixSub: Fully reset Pacman and reinstall all packages
maintenance.updateRemoteFixSub: Reset pacman.conf, mirror lists and keyring to Garuda default
maintenance.updateRemoteResetSnapperSub: Fully reset Snapper and recreate its configs
maintenance.resetTooltip: The following files or folders will be reset to their default state:
maintenance.actionFailed: An error occurred while trying to run {{ action }} with error {{ error }}.
maintenance.installationFailedHeader: {{ program }} is not installed and failed to install, cannot proceed. Check the logs for more information

sudo.title: Authentication required
sudo.subtitle: Please enter your password to continue

terminal.title: Terminal
terminal.outputExists: It seems like there is an action running already. Please wait for it to finish before trying to view logs.
terminal.clearCachedData: The output cache has been cleared.
terminal.copyToClipboard: The output has been successfully copied to your clipboard.

systemdServices.error: An error occurred while fetching services, please try again.
systemdServices.hideDeadTip: Toggle to show/hide services in 'dead', 'failed', or 'exited' state

alsa.title: ALSA
alsa.description: The foundational kernel-level sound driver. Provides the core hardware support for sound cards, though you usually interact with it through PipeWire.

jack.title: JACK
jack.description: A specialized, low-latency sound server designed for professional audio production. Usually handled natively by PipeWire now.

pulseaudio.title: PulseAudio
pulseaudio.description: The older, legacy sound server. Kept primarily for compatibility, but PipeWire has largely replaced it.

pipewire.title: PipeWire
pipewire.description: The modern standard for handling audio and video streams on Linux. It seamlessly replaces PulseAudio and JACK with much lower latency and better security.

userRealtime.title: Realtime Group
userRealtime.description: Adds your user to the realtime group, giving audio applications CPU priority to prevent stuttering and latency.

jamesdsp.title: JamesDSP
jamesdsp.description: An audio effect processor for adding bass boost, equalizers, and other effects. It is recommended to try EasyEffects first.

easyeffects.title: EasyEffects
easyeffects.description: A powerful tool to enhance your microphone quality and audio playback. Highly recommended to install 'Calf Plugins' and 'LSP Plugins' alongside this for a complete experience.

lspPlugins.title: LSP Plugins
lspPlugins.description: Linux Studio Plugins (LSP). A collection of professional audio tools like equalizers and compressors. Install this to expand EasyEffects' capabilities.

calf.title: Calf Plugins
calf.description: Calf Studio Gear. A high-quality set of audio processing plugins. Install this to give EasyEffects more professional studio effects.

qemu.title: QEMU
qemu.description: A generic, open-source machine emulator and virtualizer. Can run operating systems for any machine, on any machine.

kvm.title: KVM
kvm.description: Kernel-based Virtual Machine. Uses CPU hardware acceleration (VT-x/AMD-V) to let virtual machines run at near-native speeds.

virtManager.title: Virt Manager
virtManager.description: A powerful desktop application (GUI) for creating and managing KVM/QEMU virtual machines.

libvirtdEnabled.title: Libvirtd
libvirtdEnabled.description: The system daemon required to run and manage virtual machines and other virtualization platforms.

userVboxUsers.title: VirtualBox Users
userVboxUsers.description: Adds your user to the vboxusers group, granting permission to use VirtualBox.

virtualbox.title: VirtualBox
virtualbox.description: Oracle's popular cross-platform virtualization software. Very user-friendly, though generally slower than KVM/Virt-Manager.

userLibvirt.title: Libvirt Users
userLibvirt.description: Adds your user to the libvirt group, granting permission to manage advanced KVM/QEMU virtual machines.

userKvm.title: KVM Users
userKvm.description: Adds your user to the kvm group, granting permission to use fast, hardware-accelerated KVM virtual machines.

qemuUserStatic.title: QEMU User Static
qemuUserStatic.description: Can be used to Run foreign architecture binaries natively, i.e. via Podman

qemuUserStaticBinfmt.title: QEMU Binfmt
qemuUserStaticBinfmt.description: Register foreign arch handlers in kernel, this can be useful when attempting to run aarch64 container images.

docker.title: Docker
docker.description: The industry-standard container engine for packaging and running isolated applications.

dockerEnabled.title: Docker Service
dockerEnabled.description: Runs the background daemon required to manage and run Docker containers.

dockerCompose.title: Docker Compose
dockerCompose.description: A tool for defining and running multi-container Docker applications via configuration files.

userDocker.title: Docker Users
userDocker.description: Adds your user to the docker group, granting permission to run Docker commands without sudo.

podman.title: Podman
podman.description: A modern, daemonless alternative to Docker. It runs containers without needing root privileges, offering better security.

podmanDocker.title: Podman Docker
podmanDocker.description: A compatibility package that aliases the 'docker' command to 'podman', so existing Docker scripts work seamlessly with Podman.

podmanDesktop.title: Podman Desktop
podmanDesktop.description: A graphical application to easily manage, create, and view your Podman containers.

distrobox.title: Distrobox
distrobox.description: Creates tightly integrated containers of other Linux distributions. Lets you install and run apps from Ubuntu, Fedora, or Arch natively inside Garuda.

boxbuddy.title: BoxBuddy
boxbuddy.description: A graphical app to easily manage and use your Distrobox environments.

winboat.title: WinBoat
winboat.description: Uses containerization to provide seamlessly integrated Windows apps on Linux.

networkManager.title: NetworkManager
networkManager.description: The core daemon that configures and manages your Wi-Fi, Ethernet, and VPN connections.

networkManagerEnabled.title: NetworkManager Service
networkManagerEnabled.description: Runs the NetworkManager daemon so you can connect to the internet.

modemManagerEnabled.title: ModemManager
modemManagerEnabled.description: Runs the daemon required to connect to the internet using mobile broadband (4G/5G USB modems).

bluetooth.title: Bluetooth Support
bluetooth.description: The BlueZ Linux Bluetooth stack. Provides the core software needed to handle wireless devices.

bluetoothEnabled.title: Bluetooth Service
bluetoothEnabled.description: Runs the background service required to pair and connect to Bluetooth devices.

userBluetooth.title: Bluetooth Users
userBluetooth.description: Adds your user to the bluetooth group, granting permission to manage Bluetooth connections.

autoConnect.title: Auto Connect
autoConnect.description: Automatically reconnects to your trusted, previously paired Bluetooth devices when they power on.

autoConnectEnabled.title: Auto Connect Service
autoConnectEnabled.description: Runs the background service that actively handles automatic Bluetooth reconnections.

printingSupport.title: Printing Support
printingSupport.description: CUPS (Common Unix Printing System). The core software needed to connect and print to local or network printers.

cupsEnabled.title: CUPS Service
cupsEnabled.description: Runs the CUPS daemon required to handle print jobs and manage printers.

scanningSupport.title: SANE
scanningSupport.description: SANE (Scanner Access Now Easy). The core software needed to use flatbed and document scanners.

sanedEnabled.title: SANE Network
sanedEnabled.description: Runs the daemon that allows you to share your scanner over the local network.

userCups.title: CUPS Users
userCups.description: Adds your user to the cups group, granting permission to manage printers and print queues.

userScanner.title: Scanner Users
userScanner.description: Adds your user to the scanner group, granting permission to use document scanners.

userLp.title: LP Users
userLp.description: Adds your user to the lp (line printer) group, required by some older printing systems.

firewalld.title: Firewalld
firewalld.description: A dynamic firewall manager that uses zones to dynamically define network trust levels and rules.

firewalldEnabled.title: Firewalld Service
firewalldEnabled.description: Runs the firewalld daemon to protect your system from incoming network connections.

ufw.title: UFW
ufw.description: Uncomplicated Firewall. A simpler command-line interface for managing iptables/nftables firewall rules.

ufwEnabled.title: UFW Service
ufwEnabled.description: Runs the UFW service to enforce your simple firewall rules.

opensnitch.title: OpenSnitch
opensnitch.description: An application-level firewall that alerts you and asks for permission whenever a program tries to connect to the internet.

opensnitchEnabled.title: OpenSnitch Service
opensnitchEnabled.description: Runs the OpenSnitch daemon to monitor application network traffic.

gns.title: Garuda Nix Subsystem
gns.description: Integrates the Nix package manager, giving you access to thousands of extra software packages. Also provides a NixOS installation sharing settings with Garuda.

systemSettings.defaultDnsTip: Changes the default Domain Name System (DNS) provider for your internet connection, overriding your ISP's default. Can improve speed, privacy, or bypass censorship.
systemSettings.defaultShellTip: Changes your default terminal login shell. It's recommended to use a POSIX-compliant shell (like Bash or Zsh) and only use non-compliant shells like Fish through your terminal emulator's profile settings.
systemSettings.enableHblockTip: Enables Hblock, a script that blocks ads, trackers, and malware domains system-wide by routing their requests to a local sinkhole via your /etc/hosts file.
systemSettings.enableIwdTip: iNet Wireless Daemon (iwd). A modern, lightweight alternative to wpa_supplicant for managing Wi-Fi connections. Often connects faster and handles roaming better.

psd.title: Profile-sync-daemon
psd.description: Moves your web browser's profile (like Firefox or Brave) into fast temporary RAM (tmpfs). This significantly speeds up browser responsiveness and reduces wear on your SSD.

psdEnabled.title: PSD Service
psdEnabled.description: Runs the Profile-sync-daemon to automatically manage your browser profiles in RAM.

oomd.title: systemd-oomd
oomd.description: systemd's Out-Of-Memory daemon. It actively monitors system memory and automatically terminates memory-hogging processes before your entire computer freezes up.

guestUser.title: Guest User
guestUser.description: Creates a temporary 'guest' user account with no password, isolated from your personal files. Wiped on logout.

performanceTweaks.title: Performance Tweaks
performanceTweaks.description: Applies a set of system-level sysctl and tuning configurations to optimize Garuda for maximum responsiveness.

ananicyCpp.title: Ananicy-C++
ananicyCpp.description: Auto NICe daemon. Dynamically changes the CPU priority (niceness) of running processes, giving the active application you are currently using more CPU time so the system feels faster.

ananicyCppEnabled.title: Ananicy Service
ananicyCppEnabled.description: Runs the Ananicy daemon to continuously manage application CPU priorities.

bpftune.title: bpftune
bpftune.description: Uses eBPF to continuously monitor and automatically tune your Linux network parameters in real-time for optimal internet throughput and latency.

bpftuneEnabled.title: bpftune Service
bpftuneEnabled.description: Runs the bpftune daemon to continuously optimize your network performance.

preload.title: Preload
preload.description: A background daemon that monitors which applications you use most and pre-loads them into RAM, drastically reducing their startup time.

preloadEnabled.title: Preload Service
preloadEnabled.description: Runs the Preload daemon to cache your frequently used applications.

irqbalanceEnabled.title: IRQbalance
irqbalanceEnabled.description: Distributes hardware interrupts evenly across all your CPU cores. This prevents a single core from getting bogged down handling device signals.

powersaveTweaks.title: Powersave Tweaks
powersaveTweaks.description: Applies sysctl and configuration tweaks designed to minimize power consumption on battery-operated devices.

tlp.title: TLP
tlp.description: An advanced utility that automatically optimizes battery life on laptops by tweaking settings for Wi-Fi, disks, PCIe, and USB devices when unplugged.

tlpEnabled.title: TLP Service
tlpEnabled.description: Runs the TLP daemon to dynamically apply power-saving rules when on battery.

autoCpufreq.title: Auto-CPUfreq
autoCpufreq.description: An automatic CPU speed and power optimizer. It dynamically lowers your CPU speed when idle to save battery, and boosts it when you need performance.

autoCpufreqEnabled.title: Auto-CPUfreq Service
autoCpufreqEnabled.description: Runs the auto-cpufreq daemon to continuously manage CPU scaling.

thermald.title: thermald
thermald.description: Intel's thermal management daemon. It prevents your system from overheating by proactively using cooling methods before forcing the CPU to throttle down.

thermaldEnabled.title: thermald Service
thermaldEnabled.description: Runs the thermald daemon to monitor CPU temperatures and prevent overheating.

powerProfilesDaemon.title: Power Profiles
powerProfilesDaemon.description: Provides an API for the system to easily switch between 'Power Saver', 'Balanced', and 'Performance' operating modes.

powerProfilesDaemonEnabled.title: Power Profiles Service
powerProfilesDaemonEnabled.description: Runs the daemon that allows your desktop environment to toggle power profiles.

intelUndervolt.title: Intel Undervolt
intelUndervolt.description: Lowers the voltage supplied to Intel processors. This reduces heat and power consumption without sacrificing performance, which extends battery life and prevents thermal throttling.

intelUndervoltEnabled.title: Intel Undervolt Service
intelUndervoltEnabled.description: Runs the daemon that automatically applies your Intel undervolt settings on boot.

evdevLongPressRightClick.title: Touchpad Long Press
evdevLongPressRightClick.description: Configures libinput so that holding a single finger on a touchscreen or touchpad registers as a right-click.

evdevRce.title: Touchpad Right Click Area
evdevRce.description: Designates the bottom-right corner (or a specific area) of your touchpad to always act as a physical right-click.

audioNoSuspend.title: Audio No-Suspend
audioNoSuspend.description: Disables PipeWire's node-suspend feature. This keeps audio devices constantly active, preventing the annoying popping or clicking noises that occur when sound cards wake up from sleep.

kdeBorderlessMaximize.title: KDE Borderless Maximize
kdeBorderlessMaximize.description: A KWin tweak that removes the title bar and window borders when an application is maximized in KDE Plasma, giving you more usable vertical screen space.

fish.hint: Setting fish as default shell might cause issues with other programs. It is advised to set it as default via terminal configurations instead.

confirmation.exitApp: Are you sure you want to exit the app?
confirmation.exitAppRunningAction: Are you sure you want to exit the app? There is an action running!
confirmation.resetConfigsBody: Are you sure you want to reset the following configurations?
confirmation.applyOperationsBody: Are you sure that you want to apply all pending operations?

error.sudoPassword: This doesn't seem to be the correct password. Please try again.

systemStatus.updates: This system has pending updates (click for info)
systemStatus.pacFiles: This system has pending .pacnew files, you should check and merge those! (click for info)
systemStatus.aurUpdates: Updating AUR packages is out of scope for this application. Please use an AUR helper like Paru, and review PKGBUILD files before installation.
systemStatus.warnUpdate: Your system not been updated in 2 weeks. Please update your system to ensure security and stability.
systemStatus.learnPacdiff: If you aren't familiar with pacdiff files, inform yourself about them quickly.
systemStatus.openMaintenance: Click the button below to get started.
systemStatus.rebootNowBody: Are you sure you want to reboot now? Make sure to save all your work before proceeding.
systemStatus.warnReboot: It is strongly recommended to reboot your system after applying updates, especially when updating kernels or desktop environments.
systemStatus.snapshotCreation: A Btrfs snapshot will be created before starting the merge.
systemStatus.proceedMerge: Meld will be opened to apply changes. On the left side is the NEW file, on the right side the OLD. Move any useful changes you want to merge from the left to the right side. Click the save button or CTRL + S when done.
systemStatus.cautionMerge: Be aware that putting wrong changes into the OLD file can break your system.
systemStatus.askHelp: If you are unsure close the window without saving and ask for help on the Garuda Linux forum.

operationManager.pendingOperationsRunning: There are operations running, I'm only going to remove the ones already done.
operationManager.thisOperationRunning: Are you out of your mind? This operation is currently running!

kernels.dkmsModulesBrokenTip: Click me to schedule the installation of the broken DKMS modules

languagePacks.description: These are the language packs for your installed packages, usually providing translations for the GUI.

settings.title: App
settings.leftButtons: Show window buttons on the left

locales.description: The locales on the right are installed on your system. You can select which ones you want to keep and which ones you want to remove.

taskmanager.scriptMismatch: Script has been tampered with, refusing to execute!

success.title: Success!
success.body: Your system is set up and ready to go!

softwareSelection.body: Select the software you would like to install.

apply.body: Click 'Apply' to start the installation and configuration process.

autoSoftware.strawberryPlayer: Music player aimed at audio enthusiasts
autoSoftware.audacity: Program for manipulating digital audio waveforms
autoSoftware.museScore: Create, play and print beautiful sheet music
autoSoftware.bitwigStudio: Digital audio workstation for music production
autoSoftware.firedragon: Privacy-focused Floorp fork with Garuda branding
autoSoftware.firedragonCatppuccin: Firedragon browser with a Catppuccin theme
autoSoftware.librewolf: Privacy, security, and freedom-focused web browser
autoSoftware.chromium: Web browser built for speed, simplicity, and security
autoSoftware.ungoogledChromium: Chromium with all Google integrations removed
autoSoftware.vivaldi: Advanced browser made with the power user in mind
autoSoftware.brave: Web browser that blocks ads and trackers by default
autoSoftware.qutebrowser: Keyboard-driven, vim-like browser based on Python
autoSoftware.neochat: Client for Matrix, the decentralized communication protocol
autoSoftware.fractal: Matrix group messaging app for GNOME
autoSoftware.wire: End-to-end encrypted messenger and video conferences
autoSoftware.mumble: Low-latency, high quality voice chat software
autoSoftware.visualStudioCode: Code editor for building and debugging modern applications
autoSoftware.visualStudioCodeOss: Open source build of Visual Studio Code
autoSoftware.netbeans: IDE for Java, HTML5, PHP, C and C++
autoSoftware.postman: Build, test, and document your APIs
autoSoftware.intellijIdea: IDE for Java and other programming languages
autoSoftware.gitKraken: Intuitive, fast, and beautiful Git client
autoSoftware.github: GUI for managing Git and GitHub
autoSoftware.cockpit: Web-based user interface for Linux servers
autoSoftware.docker: Pack, ship and run applications as containers
autoSoftware.podman: Daemonless container engine for managing containers
autoSoftware.terraform: Tool for building and updating infrastructure as code
autoSoftware.mari0: Mario game with Portal gun mechanics
autoSoftware.kapman: Clone of the well known game Pac-Man
autoSoftware.kmahjongg: Tile matching game for one or two players
autoSoftware.darktable: Utility to organize and develop raw images
autoSoftware.myPaint: Fast and easy painting application for digital painters
autoSoftware.wings3d: 3D modeler using the winged edge data structure
autoSoftware.kiCad: Electronic schematic and PCB design tools
autoSoftware.xtreme: Powerful download accelerator and video downloader
autoSoftware.fdm: Powerful modern download accelerator and organizer
autoSoftware.qbittorrent: Advanced BitTorrent client based on Qt
autoSoftware.remmina: Remote desktop client written in GTK+
autoSoftware.filezilla: Fast and reliable FTP, FTPS and SFTP client
autoSoftware.warpinator: Send and receive files across the network
autoSoftware.nitroshare: Simple application to transfer files across devices
autoSoftware.linuxCachyosWithBore: Highly optimized CachyOS kernel with BORE scheduler
autoSoftware.linuxXanmod: Custom kernel with XanMod patches for performance
autoSoftware.linuxXanmodLts: LTS custom kernel with XanMod patches
autoSoftware.evolution: Manage your email, contacts and schedule
autoSoftware.easytag: Application for viewing and editing audio tags
autoSoftware.onlyOffice: Office suite combining text, spreadsheet and presentation editors
autoSoftware.obsidian: Knowledge base working on local plain text Markdown files
autoSoftware.droidcam: Turn your phone/tablet into a webcam
autoSoftware.soundwire: Transmit audio from your PC to other devices
autoSoftware.variety: Changes the wallpaper on a regular interval
autoSoftware.obsStudio: Software for live streaming and recording
autoSoftware.gnomeBoxes: Simple application to access virtual systems
autoSoftware.virtManager: Desktop user interface for managing virtual machines

desc.pacmanConf: Main configuration file for the pacman package manager
desc.grub: Configuration file for the GRUB bootloader
desc.bashrc: Bash configuration file for the current user
desc.zsh: Zsh configuration file for the current user
desc.mkinitcpio: Configuration for creating an initial ramdisk environment
desc.logind: Configuration for the systemd login manager
desc.journald: Configuration for the systemd journal service
desc.profile: User specific environment and startup programs
desc.i3: Classic X11 tiling window manager configuration
desc.os_release: Provides information about the OS, used by various programs
desc.xorg_gpu: GPU-specific Xorg configuration (driver selection, BusID, screen settings)
desc.xorg_monitor: Monitor layout, resolution, refresh rate for Xorg
desc.shadow: Contains hashed passwords and account expiration info
desc.sudoers: Configures which users have sudo privileges
desc.systemd_services: Custom or overridden systemd service definitions
desc.locale_gen: List of locales available on the system
desc.adjtime: Used by hwclock to keep track of clocks
desc.bash_bashrc: System-wide bash shell settings for interactive shells
desc.pacman_hooks: Hooks for automating actions during package operations
desc.hosts_allow: TCP wrappers for network service access control (allow)
desc.hosts_deny: TCP wrappers for network service access control (deny)
desc.udev_rules: udev rules for hardware device behavior
desc.snapperRoot: Snapper configuration for the root partition
desc.ananicy: Rules for the Ananicy auto-nice daemon
desc.cpupower: Configuration for the cpupower power management tool
desc.garudaUpdateConfig: Configuration for garuda-update behavior and update flags
desc.kwinrc: Configuration for the KWin window manager
desc.plasmarc: Main configuration for the Plasma shell
desc.plasmaEnv: Environment variables for the Plasma workspace
desc.gdm: Configuration for the GNOME Display Manager
desc.lightdm: Configuration for the LightDM display manager
desc.cinnamon: Main configuration directory for the Cinnamon desktop
desc.hyprpaper: Configuration for the Hyprpaper wallpaper utility
desc.waybar: Main configuration for the Waybar status bar
desc.waybarStyle: CSS styling for the Waybar status bar
desc.wofi: Configuration for the Wofi application launcher
desc.rofi: Configuration for the Rofi application launcher and window switcher
desc.mako: Configuration for the Mako notification daemon
desc.dunst: Configuration for the Dunst notification daemon
desc.gamemode: Global configuration for the GameMode optimization tool
desc.mangohud: Configuration for the MangoHud performance overlay
desc.pam: Configuration files for Pluggable Authentication Modules
desc.plymouthConf: Configuration for the Plymouth bootsplash daemon
desc.kvantumConfig: Configuration for the Kvantum SVG-based theme engine
desc.kdeLookandFeel: KDE Global Theme (Look and Feel) settings
desc.gtk4Css: Custom CSS styling for GTK 4.0 applications
desc.grubTheme: Configuration for the GRUB bootloader theme

bootOptions.title: Boot
bootOptions.defaultEntryHint: The menu entry that will be booted by default.
bootOptions.timeoutHint: The time in seconds to wait before booting the default entry.
bootOptions.kernelParamsHint: Additional parameters passed to the Linux kernel at boot.
bootOptions.enableBootsplashHint: Shows a graphical animation (Plymouth) during the boot process instead of text messages.
bootOptions.plymouthNotInstalled: Plymouth is not installed. Enable it to install it on apply.
bootOptions.plymouthPending: Plymouth is scheduled for installation. You can choose a theme after the installation is complete.
bootOptions.plymouthThemeHint: The theme shown during the system boot process.
bootOptions.mitigationsOffHint: Disables CPU security mitigations for better performance but less security.
bootOptions.sysrqEnabledHint: Enables the Magic SysRq key for emergency system control.
bootOptions.flatMenusHint: Disables GRUB submenus, showing all entries in the top-level menu.
bootOptions.saveDefaultHint: Automatically save the last selected boot entry as default.
bootOptions.partitionSelectHint: Please select the partition containing your Garuda installation.
bootOptions.savedMessage: Boot settings have been applied successfully.
bootOptions.savedMessageScheduled: Boot settings have been added to pending operations.

bootRepair.title: Boot
bootRepair.restoreMbrPbr: Restore MBR or PBR from backup
bootRepair.luksPasswordRequired: Please enter the LUKS password for this root partition.
